_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Bowel obstruction or bowel ischemia due to rotation of sigmoid colon about its mesentery (Sigmoid Volvulus)

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of management
X-ray erect Abdomen or Barium Enema or CT abdomen

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history especially medication history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation and/or imaging not indicative of surgery.
Clinical notes missing details on history, signs, symptoms, planned treatment, or indication for procedure.
Pre-authorization imaging (X-ray erect Abdomen or Barium Enema or CT Abdomen) does not confirm diagnosis.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and line of treatment missing or incomplete.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary missing or lacking follow-up advice at discharge.

clinical_key_pointers: Volvulus is a rotation of segment of bowel about its mesentery. Mostly occurs in >70 years old and often bedridden. Symptoms occurs due to bowel obstruction or bowel ischemia.
surgical_options: Hartmann procedure (if any evidence strangulation or perforation)
Exteriorization (Paul-Mickulicz procedure) in debilitated patients or those with impaired continence
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Nonsurgical decompression (detort by flexible sigmoidoscope or barium enema and insert rectal tube past obstruction)
